name: Girdle Scones (Scottish)
recipeIngredient: Buttermilk, or Full cream milk, or Sour milk
recipeInstructions:
1. To test the girdle's heat, sprinkle a little flour on it. If the flour browns immediately, the girdle is too hot; it should take a few seconds to turn color.
2. Sift the flour into a bowl and add the remaining dry ingredients (bicarbonate of soda, cream of tartar, baking powder). Mix well.
3. Rub in the butter until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
4. Add the golden syrup and enough milk to achieve a soft consistency when mixed with a palette knife.
5. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and roll it quickly and lightly (handle as little as possible) to about 1/4 inch thickness.
6. Cut rounds with a floured cutter or knife.
7. Grease the girdle and heat it. Place the scone rounds on the hot girdle and cook once on each side until browned.
8. Serve freshly baked, spreading butter on top.
(Note: This recipe is based on the original text provided.)
